diagrams.title: Hadoop Evolution Timeline (1999-2017)
diagrams.description: A chronological timeline showing key milestones in Hadoop's history from its origins to maturity
elements: 1999: Apache Lucene created by Doug Cutting
2004: Google publishes MapReduce paper; NDFS and MapReduce implemented
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the chronological development of Hadoop to understand its evolutionary path and key inflection points

diagrams.title: Hadoop Family Tree
diagrams.description: Shows the genealogical relationship between projects that led to Hadoop
elements: Grandparent: Apache Lucene (1999) - text search library
Parent: Apache Nutch (2002) - web search engine
Children of Nutch: NDFS (Nutch Distributed File System) and MapReduce
Great-grandchildren: HBase, Hive, Pig, Spark, Sqoop, Flume, ZooKeeper
diagrams.purpose: Understand how Hadoop emerged from earlier projects and its relationship to the broader ecosystem

diagrams.title: Hadoop Architecture Evolution
diagrams.description: Shows how Hadoop's architecture changed from version 1.0 to 3.0
elements: Hadoop 2.0: HDFS + YARN + MapReduce (YARN as resource manager)
Hadoop 3.0: Same as 2.0 + Erasure Coding + Containers + Multiple NameNodes
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the architectural changes across Hadoop versions and understand the improvements in resource management and storage efficiency
